draft.introduction: Psalm 150 is the grand doxology that closes the entire Psalter, calling on every instrument and every living thing to praise God. Its placement makes it the final word of Israel’s prayer book.

translation.text: Praise the Lord! Praise God in his sanctuary; praise him in his mighty heavens! Praise him for his mighty deeds; praise him with trumpet sound; praise him with lute and harp! Praise him with tambourine and dance; praise him with strings and pipe! Let everything that has breath praise the Lord! Praise the Lord!
translation.original_text: αἰνεῖτε τὸν Κύριον ἐν τοῖς ἁγίοις αὐτοῦ

translator_notes.note: The final verse—“Let everything that has breath praise the Lord”—extends worship beyond Israel to all living creatures. The rabbis interpreted this as including praise with every breath one takes, making all of life an act of worship.
